milestone: Story Author permission block added to the Experience Inspector
slice: The Move/Edit/Visible permission block existed for Place, Decoration, and Text panels but was never wired into the Experience Inspector at all -- a pre-existing gap the Continuous Builder sprint's new Scene Stack made far more visible, since Experience-backed rows now route there by default. Experience itself carries no .permissions field; the real permission object lives on whichever hosted object the Experience currently projects onto. Place-hosted (Frame) Experiences don't get a separate block -- already covered by the Holder's own "Can a Story Author change this? (its Frame, once populated)" checkbox. Scene/Free-hosted Experiences get the identical 3-checkbox block, reading and writing the one real mirrored Scene Layer this Experience currently has in the open Scene ("Only-one-content-type-at-a-time" guarantees at most one exists, so there's never an ambiguous instance to edit). Shown only once the Experience is actually hosted in the currently open Scene. Verified via Playwright: identical wording/behavior to the existing Place/Decoration/Text blocks; toggling a checkbox persists to the real mirrored Layer; a Place-hosted Frame Experience correctly shows no separate block of its own.
